chapter_title.en: What Garments Are Permissible for Prayer and What Garments Are Not

text: 812 - وَ رَوَى يُوسُفُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْهُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ - «لاَ بَأْسَ بِالثَّوْبِ أَنْ يَكُونَ سَدَاهُ وَ زِرُّهُ وَ عَلَمُهُ حَرِيراً وَ إِنَّمَا يُكْرَهُ اَلْحَرِيرُ اَلْمُبْهَمُ لِلرِّجَالِ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.812 - Yusuf ibn Muhammad ibn Ibrahim narrated from the Imam (as) that He said:
"There is no issue if a garment's warp, buttons, or trim is made of silk; what is disliked for men is pure silk (mubham)”.
